why_assigned: Term spans human made not natural substitute and contrived senses and therefore sits as a trajectory rather than a single point
Fake centers on non genuine deceptive or sham status and only overlaps artificial in some substitute or feigned contexts
risk: Some casual uses are less deceit loaded

why_assigned: False covers untruth deception and substitute senses and is broader than object authenticity alone
Counterfeit is imitation with intent to deceive and anchors the strongest fraud side
Sham names pretense and counterfeit display and aligns with artificial manners and tears at the feigned edge
Synthetic centers on chemically produced materials and additives and shares the not natural branch strongly
Imitation centers on copying a genuine model and often names substitute goods
Simulated centers on modeled appearance or scenario and bridges toward realism rather than raw manufacture
Realistic marks convincing resemblance to the real and often modifies artificial objects
risk: Could be misread as real if overcompressed

why_assigned: Real names actual existence or genuineness and acts as a strong contrast anchor
Genuine stresses claimed origin and sincerity and sharply opposes feigned artificial uses
risk: Can overlap authentic and real too tightly

why_assigned: Authentic adds authority and verified origin rather than mere naturalness
Natural opposes artificial in origin and often in spontaneity or food processing discourse
risk: Not every natural use is authenticity coded

why_assigned: Constructed is a neutral build term close to the core made sense of artificial
Manufactured is industrially produced and near artificial in non natural object contexts
risk: Figurative made up sense can muddy placement

why_assigned: Processed belongs to the consumer additive and industrial treatment field rather than direct imitation
risk: Consumer health discourse may overcolor it
